type_error_policy: Pycairo runtime TypeError and AttributeError assertions map to MoonBit compile-time rejection. Glyph is a closed typed value with a UInt64 index and Double coordinates; tuple subclassing maps to fields, a heterogeneous components tuple, value equality, and hashing. A runtime index operator is deliberately absent because MoonBit indexing has one static return type and converting the UInt64 index to Double would lose information. Python repr/eval behavior maps to derived structural Debug and typed construction.

test_type.adaptation: Glyph preserves its heterogeneous (UInt64, Double, Double) shape through fields and components(). Pycairo g[1] maps to destructuring components(); a homogeneous runtime index API would either erase the field type or lose UInt64 precision.

static_api: pub fn Context::glyph_extents(Self, ArrayView[@glyph.Glyph]) -> TextExtents raise CairoError
pub fn Context::glyph_path(Self, ArrayView[@glyph.Glyph]) -> Unit raise CairoError
pub fn Context::show_glyphs(Self, ArrayView[@glyph.Glyph]) -> Unit raise CairoError
test_context.adaptation: ArrayView[Glyph] statically rejects tuple/object elements. Pycairo's deprecated num_glyphs compatibility parameter is deliberately absent because MoonBit arrays carry their length.

test_glyph_limits.adaptation: UInt64 preserves the full unsigned-long range used by the supported 64-bit native targets and statically rejects negative or wider integer inputs. C marshaling additionally checks unsigned-long width on narrower ABIs.
